scores.architecture: MINIMAL-CORE: a three-verb resource core with pluggable extensions over a placement vtable
scores.note: The only submission that answers the question my lens actually asks. It DELETES the res_kind enum — the file all four recon dossiers independently flagged as 'a guaranteed four-way merge collision' — and replaces it with `res_ext {classify, derive, validate, deps_of, share_policy}` + fourcc kinds interned to a dense id. Adding .wav touches src/resources/audio/res_audio.c and nothing else: no enum, no kind_from_path switch, no routing table, no core edit. Two orthogonal seams — extensions own MEANING (spec §1 verbatim), placement owns MEMORY — and neither knows the other exists. Public surface collapses to three verbs: `ano_res_bytes_typed(read,h,tag)` (borrow, kind-gated), `ano_res_take(lt,h,&parcel)` (destructive transfer), `ano_res_derive(lt,read,src,tag)` (one door for every conditioned artifact), with ano_resgfx_model/ano_resaudio_decode/ano_resscript_compile as one-line wrappers. The parcel is the most misuse-resistant consume object of the three: it knows its own home, `ano_res_parcel_free` routes back through the recorded site, and `ano_res_parcel_zero_copy()` IS the contest's pointer-equality oracle. `res_ext.validate()` is MANDATORY and CORE-CALLED at adopt time on pack provenance — a future extension that forgets its hostile-input gate has a NULL slot the core can refuse, which is a structural guarantee neither rival has. `res_shard_alloc()` routes name/binding/dep records through the same plan() with the binding's own kind, so Model B's shard genuinely lands in its kind's metadata pool: B.1's 'changing only one payload pointer is not a contestant' is satisfied by construction. Real flaws, not hand-waved: `ano_res_parcel {void*; size_t; uint64_t token; uint32_t _site[4];}` — 16 opaque bytes that CANNOT hold their own res_site (allocator ptr + serving + alignment is already 24), so the flagship public struct is internally inconsistent AND leaks internal layout into the public header; fourcc→dense-id depends on registration order, so every stats-cube key is incomparable across builds (they admit it; the mitigation is 'discipline'); the attribution cube is (kind x lifetime x role) plus a SEPARATE per-op vector, not the full 4-way B.2 demands, so a weakness confined to one (kind x operation) cell — say promotion of audio PCM — can still hide; and RES_SLOT_MAX 4096->16384 stays a static array, deferring growability to a Stage C that will freeze it.

scores.architecture: SQUAD SOA-PLANES: placement is a plane destination, not an allocator object graph
scores.note: The best ENGINEERING and the worst factoring of the three. Correctness is the most thorough packet delivered: it is the only one that fixes the audit's own instruments — anotest_resgfx's `if (failures) return;` cascade-skip that silently voids every per-vertex ground-truth assertion the CMake comment advertises; the `#ifndef _WIN32` temp-litter blind spot on the one platform whose ReplaceFileW path most likely strands a temp; anotest_resbench's res_registry_stats() setting direct_bytes = pools.live_bytes = s.live_bytes and then asserting one against the other (every 'pooled vs direct' number ever printed is a self-comparison); and the 99.6%-stale-sentinel race whose 'never a mixed descriptor' oracle is therefore near-vacuous. Performance leads: one plane-set framing for scene/PCM/bytecode/level/font-bake/binding; planar PCM (one plane per channel) so the SoA thesis pays rent inside a decoder; telemetry cell hashed ONCE inside res_place_plan and cached in res_site.cell, so alloc/free/copy is a plain owner-thread store at a known index — Stage-C-mutex-deletion-safe by construction; and it is the ONLY submission that gives ano_mem_stripe a genuine Stage A production consumer (reader lanes — g_readers is today a static array of 16-byte lanes false-sharing across 1.2M raced reads — plus the telemetry cells), which is the exact roster rule A.2 levels at ano_mem_pool. But the modularity claim is the one that fails. The five models are 'DATA', and that data contains `int8_t kind_route[RES_KIND_COUNT][RES_ROLE_COUNT]` and `const res_arena_spec (*arena_by_key)[RES_ARENA_COUNT]` — so the KIND AXIS LIVES INSIDE THE PLACEMENT TABLE. Adding audio touches res_kind, RES_KIND_COUNT, kind_from_path, every model literal's kind_route, and every per-kind arena roster. They say so themselves: 'a guaranteed four-way merge collision — land it once, early.' That is precisely the god-table my lens penalizes: descriptive vocabulary that drives behavior, welded to a core enum. And `plan_hook` is an unenforced escape hatch in the contest's routing function, guarded by 'a POLICY... enforced by review, not by the compiler' — in a contest whose entire premise is that no model may hide an allocator, a review-only guard on plan() is the weakest link in an otherwise superb design. Usability: preserves res_owned_alloc's exact signature (zero call-site churn — correct), but `ano_res_transfer_free(void*)` needs a global lookup with no compile-time link to its owner, and it narrows the public path alphabet by TWO bytes ('#' AND '@').

One genuinely first-class idea buried under the most ceremony. The idea: mint REFUSES, so illegal placement is unrepresentable rather than caught late — `res_cap res_cap_mint(const res_cap_req*)` returns the null capability when no arena the active model offers can deliver req->alignment, which structurally kills the live bug where owned_alloc_locked passes size to ano_mem_multipool_alloc and DROPS plan->alignment, silently serving a 20-byte ANO_CACHE_LINE request from the 32-byte class. Keep that refusal. Discard the token that carries it. The tax: `res_owned_alloc(res_cap, size, out)`, `res_owned_stage(res_cap,...)`, `res_owned_commit(staged, res_cap home, out)`, `res_owned_move(from, res_cap to, out)`, `res_account_copy(res_cap, bytes)` — every internal verb re-signatured, so every extension author now writes mint → check-valid → alloc → check-fail, and 'that verbosity tax is real' is their own words. Compare: both rivals keep `res_owned_alloc(const res_place_plan*, size_t, res_owned_block*)` and change zero call sites. Worse, the token is bit-packed — `uint16_t axes; // kind:5 | role:3 | op:4 | dest:4` — so the design ships with a hard ceiling of 32 kinds and EIGHT ROLES against seven already defined. A new role class does not extend this architecture; it breaks its ABI. That is the opposite of what I am scoring for. And the ledger is the gate failure: charges are interned into `RES_CHARGE_MAX = 256` cells with everything past that dumped into 'the overflow cell 0'. The realized (kind x lifetime x role x op) tuple space today is already large; the moment audio, script, level, pack, binding and font-bake land, attribution degrades to 'overflow' EXACTLY WHEN THE NEW DOMAINS ARRIVE — and B.2's whole purpose is that 'aggregate wins cannot hide one catastrophically weak class'. A contest cube that coarsens under the classes it exists to expose is not evidence. Finally, they concede the shared-immutable alias edge 'is a refcount wearing a nicer hat... I have removed the silently, not the counting' — honest, and the closest any submission comes to the A.4 bullet 7 prohibition. The five-model proof is excellent and real C; the seam underneath (res_placement vtable + res_backing + res_free_mode) is essentially identical to MINIMAL-CORE's, which is the tell: the capability adds nothing the vtable did not already have, and costs a 16-byte token in every block on top of a ~32-byte site.

winner: MINIMAL-CORE: a three-verb resource core with pluggable extensions over a placement vtable
rationale:
All three clear the correctness gate and all three genuinely host five models — I checked, and I will not manufacture a fatal flaw to make the ranking tidier. Each proves it the same way: a plan/alloc/free vtable, a res_site that remembers its arena so a free routes home, and a wink flag (RES_BACKING_WINKS_WHOLE / res_free_mode / needs_teardown_records) that lets C and E destroy one heap while A, B and D pay an honest retail walk. Model A cannot masquerade as Model C in any of them, because none of them lets a model acquire memory except through plan(). That is the real convergent discovery of this contest and it should be recorded as settled.

So the decision falls to my lens, and there it is not close. The question is: which design makes adding a resource class NOT touch the core? SOA-PLANES answers 'it touches res_kind, RES_KIND_COUNT, kind_from_path, and every model's kind_route[RES_KIND_COUNT][RES_ROLE_COUNT]' — and says so, calling it a guaranteed four-way merge collision. CAPABILITY answers 'it touches res_kind, kind_from_path, the authority tables, and if you need an eighth role it breaks a bitfield'. MINIMAL-CORE answers: it touches one new TU. `res_ext_register()` + fourcc kinds + core-dispatched classify/derive/validate/deps_of DELETES the res_kind enum outright. That is the single highest-value structural act available in this codebase, because that enum is where four squads were told, in four independently written recon dossiers, that they would collide.

The seam count decides the rest. MINIMAL-CORE has exactly two, and they are orthogonal: extensions own meaning, placement owns memory, and neither can name the other. SOA-PLANES fuses them — its five model literals carry a per-kind routing dimension, so the allocator contest and the resource-class roster are welded to the same table, and a squad adding audio is editing Model E's data. CAPABILITY fuses them into a token that every allocation site must mint and every verb must carry, which is ceremony: a 16-byte value threaded through res_owned_alloc/stage/commit/move/free plus a refusal branch at each, buying a gate that a `static const legal[role][dest]` check inside plan() delivers for free.

And the consume model. MINIMAL-CORE's `ano_res_take(lt, h, &parcel)` / `ano_res_parcel_free(&parcel)` / `ano_res_parcel_zero_copy(&parcel)` is the only one where the transferred block knows its own home. SOA-PLANES hands out `ano_res_transfer_free(void*)` against a global side table. CAPABILITY hands out `ano_res_transfer_return(lifetime, p, size)` — making the caller re-state two facts the manager already knows, where a wrong size silently corrupts the ledger and a wrong lifetime frees into the wrong heap. That is a misuse magnet in the one API that exists to make hand-off explicit.

MINIMAL-CORE loses performance points honestly: no plane thesis, a static 128 KiB directory, a 3-axis cube with a real (kind x operation) blind spot, and — its own bug — a public `ano_res_parcel` whose 16 opaque bytes cannot hold the ~32-byte res_site it claims to carry. Those are exactly the holes the grafts fill, and every one of them is fixable inside MINIMAL-CORE's two seams without disturbing them. The reverse is not true: you cannot graft res_ext into SOA-PLANES without dissolving the kind axis out of its five model literals, i.e. redesigning its headline artifact.

A competent C engineer six months from now, handed a new resource class, opens one file under MINIMAL-CORE and three plus a merge conflict under either rival. That is the whole judgment.
grafts: FROM SOA-PLANES — res_block framing, as the shared implementation behind res_ext.validate(). MINIMAL-CORE mandates validate() but leaves every extension to hand-roll its own extent math, which is six independent chances to rewrite the same OOB bug. Take res_block_hdr {magic, version, layout_id, block_hash, plane_count, off[]}, res_plane_layout() as a PURE function of (hdr_bytes, count, elem_size[], n_planes), and res_block_open() as the one framing gate (magic/version/layout_id/block_hash/plane extents/grain). Extensions then supply only the cross-reference pass — prim.material, node.mesh, node.parent, child_first+child_count, children[i], roots[i], indices[i] — which is the part only they can know. This is the highest-severity item in the entire ledger (ano_resgfx_scene bounds-checks EXTENTS and validates NOTHING INSIDE THEM, safe today only because cgltf constructs the indices; a baked block from a pack turns every one into an OOB-read primitive handed to the renderer), and it must be fixed ONCE, not six times. Take RES_PLANE_GRAIN=64 as a compile-time constant deliberately decoupled from ANO_CACHE_LINE, with the reason stated: a baked block's interior offsets are an ABI and may not vary with the target's 128-byte line on arm64.

FROM SOA-PLANES — the telemetry cell, cached in the site. Intern the full (kind, lifetime, role, operation, destination) tuple ONCE inside plan() and store the cell index in res_site; charge alloc/free/copy/transfer/promote with a plain non-atomic store at a known index. This simultaneously closes MINIMAL-CORE's admitted (kind x operation) attribution hole, avoids CAPABILITY's 256-cell overflow bucket, keeps the hot path to one cache line, and is owner-thread-only BY CONSTRUCTION — so Stage C's deletion of g_reg.mtx does not silently turn the counter cube into a data race. Size the cell table against the RECORDED tuple population from a full level cycle, not a guess. This is the single best idea in the SOA-PLANES packet and it is orthogonal to everything MINIMAL-CORE does.

FROM SOA-PLANES — planar payload layout where it pays rent, which is what makes ano_mem_stripe legitimate in Stage A. Audio PCM conditioned to one f32 plane PER CHANNEL (the mixer walks one plane per channel — the SoA thesis paying rent inside a decoder), scene plane-sets, the GPU binding table as {geometry_pool_index | material_index | bindless_index} planes. Then take the two Stage A stripe consumers CAPABILITY explicitly concedes it does not have and MINIMAL-CORE only reaches in Stage B: the reader lanes (g_readers[64] is a static array of 16-byte lanes false-sharing across 1.2M raced reads in anotest_resownership — a real, measurable win, not a synthetic one) and the telemetry cell planes. Without this graft the winner ships a fourth allocator whose only consumers are Models D and E, which is verbatim the charge A.2 'Allocator roster' levels at ano_mem_pool today.

FROM SOA-PLANES — chunked permanent identity planes, or at minimum the evidence discipline behind them. Take rid2 unconditionally (it is what makes shard-owned names sound: row_bind's collision refusal memcmps row->name on a row whose payload may already be retired, so {rid, rid2, name_len} comparison is a PREREQUISITE, not a follow-up). Take the chunked directory only if the bench says the plane_dir[slot>>12][slot&4095] indirection on the hottest read is free — SOA-PLANES admits it has not measured this. Take unconditionally: gather peak rows/domains/readers across a full level cycle and record them in docs/benchmarks/ BEFORE Stage C freezes the publication directory, because growing a static array of atomic pointers after that point is a redesign, not a constant bump.

FROM CAPABILITY — mint-time refusal, demoted from a token to a precondition inside plan(). Keep the semantic ('illegal placement is unrepresentable, not merely rejected late'); throw away the 16-byte res_cap, the per-callsite mint, and the kind:5|role:3|op:4|dest:4 bitfield that caps the engine at eight roles. Concretely: res_site.alignment reports what the arena WILL DELIVER, plan() REFUSES rather than under-deliver, and a static const legal[RES_ROLE_COUNT][RES_DEST_COUNT] table is checked by the core before it calls the model. This kills the live alignment lie at resources_registry.c:160-165, where owned_alloc_locked passes size to ano_mem_multipool_alloc and drops plan->alignment entirely — a 20-byte payload asking for ANO_CACHE_LINE is served from the 32-byte class at 32-byte alignment, which is currently hidden only because res_read_all's staging buffer happens to be cache-aligned, and which will silently poison any Model D/E stripe claim the moment direct landing lands.

FROM CAPABILITY — the loud outstanding-transfer report. MINIMAL-CORE already has the right teardown barrier (RES_SITE_OWN_HEAP + res_backing.outstanding_parcels + a retryable -2 from ano_res_domain_retire, which correctly reuses the -2 stalled-reader idiom the codebase already speaks). Graft SOA-PLANES/CAPABILITY's shutdown behavior on top: an ERROR at shutdown naming EVERY outstanding parcel. The honest resolution of the transfer-vs-wink contradiction is to convert a silent double-free hazard into a loud accounted leak; say so in the header, and make the leak audible.

FIX MINIMAL-CORE'S OWN BUG BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE SHIPS. `typedef struct ano_res_parcel { void *data; size_t size; uint64_t token; uint32_t _site[4]; }` — those 16 opaque bytes cannot hold a res_site (allocator pointer + serving + alignment is already 24 bytes, before arena/backing/flags). Delete _site[4] entirely and let the uint64_t token index an owner-side parcel table, which the design needs anyway for outstanding_parcels and the shutdown leak report. This also stops internal placement layout from leaking into include/anoptic_resources.h, which the neutral-interface requirement (A.4 bullet 2) is supposed to be verifiable by diffing.

FIX MINIMAL-CORE'S FOURCC ORDERING. Sort the registered kind table by fourcc at res_freeze() so the dense runtime id is a deterministic function of the tags, not of res_ext_register() call order. MINIMAL-CORE concedes that stats-cube keys, memory dumps and bench tables are otherwise incomparable across builds and offers 'a fixed res_ext_all[] list -- a discipline, not a guarantee'. A sort is a guarantee and costs one line.

FROM SOA-PLANES — the derived-key decision, but keep MINIMAL-CORE's version, which is strictly better and should be stated as the winner's law. SOA-PLANES and CAPABILITY both reserve TWO path bytes ('#' and '@') and build derived keys by string concatenation through res_key_derived(). MINIMAL-CORE bans ONE byte and computes res_rid_derived(src_rid, kind_tag) = fnv1a64_seeded(src_rid ^ tag) — no concatenation, no key-length limit, no '@' namespace narrowing, and it drops straight into the pack TOC, which is rid-keyed already. Make duplicate identities use the same seeded scheme (seed on the owner index) instead of the '<logical>@<owner>' string MINIMAL-CORE still carries, and the reserved-byte cost falls to exactly one character.

FROM SOA-PLANES — migration step 0 and the instrument repairs, which are not architecture but without which the contest is judged on fiction. (1) The audit report's 'red tree' is STALE: orphan recovery is implemented at resources_registry.c:1327-1360 and anotest_persistence.c zero-inits its operand; record the TRUE baseline with commit sha + platform + raw log path before anyone claims a regression. (2) anotest_resbench's res_registry_stats() sets direct_bytes = s.live_bytes AND pools.live_bytes = s.live_bytes, so its 'chunks +N, direct +N' split and its two closing assertions compare a number against itself — no figure from that shim may enter the contest tables. (3) anotest_resgfx's check_scene() early-outs on the GLOBAL failures counter, silently skipping every per-vertex/node/material ground-truth assertion the CMake comment advertises. (4) The no-temp-litter oracle is #ifndef _WIN32'd out on the exact platform whose ReplaceFileW/MoveFileExW path is likeliest to strand a temp — replace the dirent blocks with rmos_scan_dir. (5) anotest_resownership's publication race observes 99.6% stale sentinels, so its 'never a mixed descriptor' oracle is near-vacuous; rotate >=8 live resources with a dwell window and ASSERT a coverage floor so a regression to vacuity fails.
fatal_flaws: NONE of the three fails the five-model test, and I will not invent one to sharpen the ranking. All three prove it with real code over the same fundamental shape — a plan/alloc/free vtable, a res_site that remembers its arena so a free routes home without re-derivation, and a per-model flag (RES_BACKING_WINKS_WHOLE / res_free_mode RETAIL-vs-WINK / needs_teardown_records) that lets C and E retire by one mi_heap_destroy while A, B and D pay an honest retail walk they cannot outsource. In all three, Model A is structurally incapable of masquerading as Model C, because the core acquires no memory except through plan(). That convergence is the contest's real finding and should be recorded as settled before Stage B.

CAPABILITY — NEAR-FATAL, ATTRIBUTION GATE. `RES_CHARGE_MAX = 256` interned ledger cells with everything beyond dumped into 'the overflow cell 0'. B.2 exists so that 'aggregate wins cannot hide one catastrophically weak class'. The realized (kind x lifetime x role x operation) tuple space will cross 256 precisely when audio, script, level, pack, graphics-binding and font-bake land — i.e. the cube coarsens exactly when the classes it was built to expose arrive. A contest judged on a cube that degrades under load is not judging on evidence. Repairable by sizing against recorded population, but it must be named before anyone quotes a number from it.

CAPABILITY — NEAR-FATAL, EXTENSION CEILING. `uint16_t axes; // kind:5 | role:3 | op:4 | dest:4`. Five bits of kind (32 max, 19 consumed by the ledger's own additions) and THREE BITS OF ROLE against seven roles already defined in resources_internal.h:52-60. An eighth role class does not extend this architecture; it breaks the ABI of the token that gates every allocation in the module. A design whose central artifact caps the vocabulary it exists to carry cannot win a modularity contest.

CAPABILITY — SELF-CONFESSED. 'The shared-immutable alias edge is a refcount wearing a nicer hat, and I will not pretend otherwise... I have removed the silently, not the counting.' A.4 bullet 7 says 'without silently adding reference counts'. Their edge is named, charged, per-borrower and makes retirement REFUSE with an ERROR naming the borrowers, so it is arguably compliant — but it is the closest any submission comes to a stated prohibition, and the owner must rule on it explicitly rather than let it pass on the strength of an adverb.

SOA-PLANES — NEAR-FATAL, CONTEST INTEGRITY. `int (*plan_hook)(const res_model *, const res_place_plan *, size_t, res_site *inout);` in the model literal, guarded by their own words: 'Mitigation is a POLICY (a hook may only NARROW a site the table already produced; it cannot invent a root), enforced by review, not by the compiler. A policy is weaker than an invariant and we are not pretending otherwise.' The entire premise of Stage B is that no contestant may hide an allocator or launder a wound. A review-only escape hatch installed in the routing function is the one place that premise cannot be defended by construction. If SOA-PLANES's placement layer is adopted in any form, plan_hook must be deleted, not policed.

SOA-PLANES — STRUCTURAL, AND THE REASON IT LOSES. The kind axis lives INSIDE the placement data: `int8_t kind_route[RES_KIND_COUNT][RES_ROLE_COUNT]` and `const res_arena_spec (*arena_by_key)[RES_ARENA_COUNT]` are fields of res_model. Adding a resource class therefore edits res_kind, RES_KIND_COUNT, kind_from_path, every model literal's routing table and every per-kind arena roster — welding the resource-class roster to the allocator contest. Their own migration step 1 calls it 'a guaranteed four-way merge collision -- land it once, early'. Landing it once does not make it modular; it makes it a scheduling constraint on four squads.

MINIMAL-CORE — PUBLIC-HEADER BUG IN ITS FLAGSHIP VERB. `typedef struct ano_res_parcel { void *data; size_t size; uint64_t token; uint32_t _site[4]; } ano_res_parcel;` — 16 opaque bytes purporting to carry a res_site whose own declaration ({void *allocator; uint16_t arena; uint8_t backing; uint8_t flags; size_t serving; size_t alignment;}) is at least 32. The struct cannot hold what its comment says it holds, and it leaks internal placement layout into include/anoptic_resources.h, which the neutral-interface requirement is supposed to be verifiable by diffing. Not fatal — the token alone suffices, indexing an owner-side parcel table the design needs anyway — but it must be fixed before the header is written, not after a consumer depends on it.
PROCESS — the packet contains THREE architectures, not four. The fourth squad's submission is absent. I have scored what was delivered; I have not inferred, reconstructed, or credited what was not.

scores.architecture: SQUAD SOA-PLANES: placement is a plane destination, not an allocator object graph
scores.note: Only design whose Model B is ACTUALLY kind-major. Its shard is a plane of per-binding `res_bind *` (`res_bind * *plane_bind[C]`), so each binding is individually routed by `site.root = root_index_for_key(plan->kind)`. CAPABILITY (`res_shard_row **shard_chunks`) and MINIMAL-CORE (`res_binding *chunks[RES_SHARD_CHUNKS]`, 32-binding chunks) both allocate the shard in CHUNKS of mixed-kind bindings — so under B, a 32-row chunk lands in ONE kind's metadata arena while holding rows of many kinds. That is B.4's forbidden 'a tag in the Model A pool', wearing a kind-major hat. Only SOA-PLANES structurally avoids it. It is also the only design that satisfies A.2 'Allocator roster' for `ano_mem_stripe` in Stage A: it gives it TWO verified production consumers (the 16-byte `g_readers` lanes that false-share 4-to-a-line today, and the telemetry cell planes) plus audio PCM channel planes and the GPU binding table. Correctness gates: alias bug closed by REFUSE-by-default (`ano_res_get(lt,l) == ano_res_get_ex(lt,l,ANO_RES_SHARE_REFUSE)`); SHARED_IMMUTABLE is engine-lived and refuses retirement while any domain lives — the only NO-COUNT answer to A.4 bullet 7 in the packet; ONE `res_block_open` hostile-input gate (magic/version/layout_id/block_hash/plane extents) + per-extension cross-reference pass hoisted to load time, which closes the highest-severity hole in the ledger (prim.material/node.mesh/children[i]/indices[i] are unchecked today); `republish_locked` single release-store, never NULL. Wounds I hold against it: `plan_hook` is a review-enforced-only escape that can smuggle a model an advantage (B.2: 'a hidden allocator cannot absorb a model's predicted wound'); the `res_kind` enum and `kind_from_path`'s switch — the two guaranteed four-way merge collisions — SURVIVE; migration step 9 widens the vertex layout BEFORE step 10 retains handles, exactly backwards from the recon's explicit sequencing warning.

scores.architecture: MINIMAL-CORE: a three-verb resource core with pluggable extensions over a placement vtable
scores.note: Best structure in the packet and it loses on my lens. `res_ext_register` + fourcc kinds + dense interning KILLS the res_kind enum and kind_from_path's switch — the two files the recon says four squads are guaranteed to collide on. `res_rid_derived(src_rid, kind_tag)` is the single best primitive submitted: a derived resource has NO string key at all, so type confusion is unrepresentable without narrowing the path alphabet. `ano_res_parcel{data,size,token,_site[4]}` + `ano_res_parcel_zero_copy()` hands the contest its pointer-equality oracle for free, and `outstanding_parcels` as a retire BARRIER (-2 while a parcel flies) is the cleanest resolution of the transfer/wink-out contradiction anywhere in the packet. But it fails gates. (1) Its telemetry is a dense (kind x lifetime x role) cube plus a SEPARATE flat per-op vector — it admits 'a weakness that exists only in one (kind x operation) cell — say, promotion of audio PCM specifically — can still hide.' B.2 requires four-axis attribution precisely 'so aggregate wins cannot hide one catastrophically weak class.' It fails the contest gate that Stage A exists to erect. (2) Having banned '#' it then mints DUPLICATE keys as the string `"<logical>@<owner>"` with '@' still legal in `byte_ok` — reopening the exact collision/type-confusion hole `res_rid_derived` was invented to close, three paragraphs after closing it. (3) Model B's chunked shard (above). (4) `res_placement_b_retire_kind(kind) = mi_heap_destroy(g_b[kind].heap)` is claimed to run 'through the same vtable'; it is a bespoke exported symbol that bypasses publication invalidation and the epoch grace barrier — a use-after-free against any live reader. (5) `e_wink`'s `else e_defer_transfer_heap(b)` branch is dead if retire already returns -2 on outstanding parcels; two mechanisms, contradictory. (6) Dense kind ids are registration-order-dependent, so its own contest tables are not comparable across builds.

The strongest auditability thesis — 'a capability that cannot be charged cannot be minted, so an unattributed byte is unrepresentable' — and `res_cap_transition` as the ONLY cross-domain mover, billing the DESTINATION's ledger cell so a promotion's bytes can never be charged to the source, is the most precise accounting rule submitted. `res_free_mode {RETAIL, WINK}` passed into `placement->free()` puts the teardown shape in the MODEL rather than in a core `if`, which is more correct than SOA-PLANES's `res_place_domain_winkable()` core branch. But: (1) `res_cap_mint` claims to refuse when 'NO arena the active placement offers for this tuple can deliver req->alignment' — and `res_cap_req` has NO SIZE FIELD, while `ano_mem_multipool_alloc(mp,size)` aligns to min(class stride, 4096), a function of the size class. The mint-time alignment refusal is therefore unsound; it is duplicated (correctly) at plan time anyway, where every model ends `return o->alignment >= res_cap_alignment(c) ? 0 : -1;`. Delete the mint-time check or it will refuse legal plans and admit illegal ones. (2) `c_open` does `c_dom *d = mi_heap_calloc(root, 1, sizeof *d)` and `c_wink` does `mi_heap_destroy(b->root); b->root = NULL;` and THEN conditionally touches `b->transfer_root` — `b->model_state` is now dangling and is never nulled; any later `arena_stats` or deferred-transfer path is a UAF. (3) `uint16_t axes` = kind:5|role:3|op:4|dest:4 hard-caps roles at 8 — and the tree already has 7 — and kinds at 32, with 19 already claimed. A hard undeclared ceiling, static_asserted into a 16-byte token. (4) It ships `ano_mem_stripe` and then admits 'under the two TRUTHFUL scaffolds nothing routes to it — it is a roster item without a Stage-A production consumer, which is precisely what A.2 Allocator roster complains about for ano_mem_pool.' That is a self-declared violation of a live spec rule; SOA-PLANES found real consumers for it. (5) Self-admitted 'verbosity tax': every ingest site gains a refusable mint, so failure paths multiply across the module — the worst implementer ergonomics of the three.

winner: SQUAD SOA-PLANES: placement is a plane destination, not an allocator object graph
rationale:
First, a factual note the packet does not make: FOUR architectures were promised, THREE were delivered. I judged the three that exist.

None of the three fails the five-model gate outright. All three can host complete A/B/C/D/E with registry shards owned by the model, none secretly forces a single shape, and all three correctly derive transferability as an OUTPUT of placement rather than the caller-computed `size + 1 > RES_POOL_MAX` that is duplicated at three sites today. All three independently found the same three real bugs I verified in the tree — the missing lifetime comparison at `ano_res_get`'s hit path, the dropped `plan->alignment` on the pooled branch, and the fact that `ano_res_release`'s store-NULL-then-restore would break old-complete-or-new-complete if reused for reload. That is a strong field.

SOA-PLANES wins on correctness, which is the gate.

The decisive discriminator is Model B. B.4 forbids a kind from being 'a tag in the Model A pool' — a kind must be a REAL backing domain. CAPABILITY allocates the registry shard as `res_shard_row **shard_chunks`; MINIMAL-CORE as `res_binding *chunks[RES_SHARD_CHUNKS]` in 32-binding chunks. A chunk has ONE allocating capability and therefore ONE kind — but it holds bindings of MANY kinds. So under Model B, both designs' 'kind-major registry' is a chunk of mixed-kind rows sitting in one arbitrary kind's metadata pool. That is precisely the tag-in-a-shared-pool that B.4 outlaws, and neither squad noticed. SOA-PLANES's `res_bind * *plane_bind[C]` is a plane of per-binding pointers, so each `res_bind` is individually routed by `site.root = root_index_for_key(plan->kind)` and Model B's shard is genuinely kind-major. It is the only submission whose Model B is not a fiction.

Second: A.2 'Allocator roster' says every shipped allocator gets a real resource consumer and a home benchmark, and names `ano_mem_pool`'s absent consumer as an existing sin. CAPABILITY ships `ano_mem_stripe` and openly states nothing routes to it in Stage A. MINIMAL-CORE ships it only for D/E. SOA-PLANES gives it two verified Stage-A consumers — and one of them is a real bug fix: `g_readers` is 64 lanes of `{_Atomic uint32_t; _Atomic uint64_t}` = 16 bytes, four to a cache line, hammered by 1.2M raced reads in anotest_resownership. No other squad found it. That is the difference between a roster entry and an allocator that pays rent.

Third: SOA-PLANES's answer to A.4 bullet 7 is the only one with no counting in it. SHARED_IMMUTABLE is engine-lived; retiring it while any domain is live is refused. An alias into a domain that cannot die is not a lifetime hazard and needs no count to prove it. CAPABILITY's alias edge is, by its own words, 'a refcount wearing a nicer hat' — it removed the word 'silently', not the counting.

Fourth, the `res_block_open` bet. One framing, one validator, one bake path, one pack entry type, one hostile-input battery for scene/PCM/bytecode/level/font-bake/binding. It is a concentrated blast radius and SOA-PLANES names it as such — but the alternative is six separate validators for the sharpest hole in the ledger (`prim.material`, `node.mesh`, `node.parent`, `children[i]`, `roots[i]`, `indices[i]` are ALL unchecked today, safe only because cgltf constructed them; the bake turns every one into an OOB read primitive handed to the renderer). One function that is the most fuzz-worthy in the module beats six that are each 'probably fine'.

What it loses on: it keeps the `res_kind` enum and `kind_from_path`'s switch — the two guaranteed four-way merge collisions — where MINIMAL-CORE deletes both. Its `plan_hook` is a compiler-unenforceable escape in exactly the place B.2 says a hidden allocator must not be able to absorb a model's wound. And its migration widens the vertex layout at step 9 and only retains renderer handles at step 10 — backwards from the recon's explicit warning that those two changes will fight in the same files. All three are fixable by graft, and none of them is a correctness gate. MINIMAL-CORE's telemetry cube, by contrast, cannot be grafted away — it is architectural, and it fails B.2's four-axis rule by its author's own admission.

So: SOA-PLANES wins the gate. It must be rebuilt on MINIMAL-CORE's extension registry and derived-identity primitives before it ships, and it must adopt CAPABILITY's free-mode and ledger-cell rules. Without those grafts it is the right skeleton with the wrong organs.
grafts: FROM MINIMAL-CORE — `res_ext` registry + fourcc kinds + dense interning. Delete SOA-PLANES's `res_kind` enum and `kind_from_path`'s switch outright. `res_ext{classify, derive, validate, deps_of, share_policy}` with `res_ext_kind{uint32_t tag /* ANO_FOURCC('R','G','F','X') */, name, bool derived}` makes a new resource class ONE TU and removes the two guaranteed four-way merge collisions the recon flags in both `resources_internal.h` and `resources_registry.c:744`. Two mandatory corrections to MINIMAL-CORE's version: pin the dense id to a fixed static `res_ext_all[]` order (their own wound: registration-order-dependent ids make the contest's own stats cube incomparable across builds), and NEVER let a dense id reach disk, a pack TOC, or a bake header — fourcc only.

FROM MINIMAL-CORE — `res_rid_derived(uint64_t src_rid, uint32_t kind_tag)` as the derived-identity primitive. A derived resource gets NO string key at all: its rid is a seeded hash of (source rid, kind tag). This is strictly stronger than SOA-PLANES's reserved-byte grammar, because it makes filesystem/derived type confusion unrepresentable rather than merely refused — AND it lets SOA-PLANES retract its self-declared wound of narrowing the public path alphabet ('a user mod or asset pack with # in a filename becomes unloadable'). Keep SOA-PLANES's `res_key_validate` + the `plane_kind[]` identity record + kind-checked typed accessors as the second layer.

COROLLARY GRAFT, AND IT FIXES MINIMAL-CORE'S OWN BUG — apply `res_rid_derived` to DUPLICATE keys too. MINIMAL-CORE mints `"<logical>@<owner>"` as a string while leaving '@' legal in `byte_ok`, reopening the exact collision hole it just closed; SOA-PLANES mints `"<logical>@<owner_index>"` and bans '@'. Neither is right. A duplicate identity should be `res_rid_duplicate(src_rid, owner_index)` — no string, no reserved byte, no namespace narrowing. SOA-PLANES's decision that owner INDEXES (not generations) key the duplicate — so the permanent identity cost is bounded at RES_DOMAIN_MAX per logical path forever — is the correct half and must survive.

FROM MINIMAL-CORE — `ano_res_derive(lifetime, read, anores_t src, uint32_t kind_tag)` as the ONE adoption door, with `ano_resgfx_model`/`ano_resgfx_image_decoded`/`ano_resaudio_decode`/`ano_resscript_compile`/`ano_restext_bake` as one-line wrappers over it. SOA-PLANES has six bespoke ingest entry points; one door means one `validate()` call site, one dependency-disclosure site, one accounting site, and one place where a hostile block can enter. This is what makes the `res_block_open` concentration defensible instead of merely centralized.

FROM MINIMAL-CORE — `ano_res_parcel{void *data; size_t size; uint64_t token; uint32_t _site[4];}` + `ano_res_take()` + `ano_res_parcel_free()` + `ano_res_parcel_zero_copy()`. SOA-PLANES's `ano_res_transfer_free(void *data)` must look the bare pointer up in a side transfer plane on every return; the parcel carries its own site home. And `ano_res_parcel_zero_copy()` hands the contest B.6's 'zero-copy rate across the full size distribution' oracle for free, where SOA-PLANES has to infer it from `releases_zero_copy`/`releases_copied` counters.

FROM MINIMAL-CORE — `res_backing.outstanding_parcels` as a RETIRE BARRIER: `ano_res_domain_retire` returns -2 while a parcel is in flight, the same retryable shape `ano_res_shutdown` already uses for stalled readers. SOA-PLANES's process-lived `res_placement.transfer_heap` turns a forgotten transfer into a permanent leak that is merely LOUD; the barrier turns it into a refusal the caller must handle. Keep SOA-PLANES's shutdown ERROR naming every outstanding block on top of it — belt and braces. (Note both squads' own contradiction: MINIMAL-CORE's `e_wink` has an `else e_defer_transfer_heap(b)` branch that is dead once the -2 barrier exists. Pick the barrier; delete the deferral.)

FROM CAPABILITY — `res_free_mode {RES_FREE_RETAIL, RES_FREE_WINK}` passed INTO `placement->free()`. SOA-PLANES branches once in the CORE on `res_place_domain_winkable(lt)`, which puts model knowledge in the registry and cannot express a domain with MIXED arenas — which is exactly Model E (winkable lifetime root + non-winkable transfer heap). Passing the mode to the model lets it decide per-SITE (`if (m == RES_FREE_WINK && (st->flags & RES_SITE_WINKABLE)) return;`), which is both more correct and more honest.

FROM CAPABILITY — the Debug post-wink assertion: after every `wink()`, assert the domain's `live_bytes` returned to zero. A model that lies about `winkable`/`WINKS_WHOLE` then fails LOUDLY instead of double-freeing quietly. SOA-PLANES has no such gate and its `needs_teardown_records` flag is exactly the kind of model-declared claim that needs one.

FROM CAPABILITY — `res_cap_transition` as the ONLY cross-domain mover, and its rule that the destination names a DISTINCT ledger cell so a promotion's bytes CAN NEVER be billed to the source. SOA-PLANES's `ano_res_promote` says it charges 'exactly one copies/bytes_copied/promotions' and never says to whose telemetry cell. B.5/B.7 judge C and E on promotion cost; if promotion bytes can land in the source's cell, the marquee number is fiction. Also take CAPABILITY's rule that `res_disposition_allowed` becomes a PURE predicate — today it bumps `stats.promotions`/`stats.duplications` when a TEST merely asks whether a move would be legal, so every promotion number in the tree currently counts questions, not events.

FIX IN ALL THREE, MANDATORY IN THE WINNER — Model B's whole-kind teardown. SOA-PLANES asserts 'whole-kind teardown (subsystem restart) IS a mi_heap_destroy'; MINIMAL-CORE exports `res_placement_b_retire_kind()` and falsely claims it goes 'through the same vtable'; CAPABILITY exports `res_place_b_kind_wink()`. All three destroy a kind's root heap WITHOUT invalidating publication and WITHOUT crossing the epoch grace barrier — a use-after-free against any reader inside an open `ano_res_read` scope. It must run the identical invalidate -> `queue_retire_locked` -> `reader_safe` -> `collect_locked` sequence that `ano_res_domain_retire` runs, or Model B's home-ground number is measured on a crash. CAPABILITY's scoping ('the contest harness calls this, production never does') is the only honest framing and should be adopted verbatim alongside the fix.

FROM MINIMAL-CORE — the migration sequencing. Renderer retains handles (M7: `parseGltf` -> `ano_render_bind_scene`, ModelAsset deleted) lands BEFORE the vertex-layout widening (M8), 'so the renderer is already on views and only one file fights.' SOA-PLANES has it backwards — step 9 widens `anoresgfx_vertex` (forcing `vertex.h`, the static_asserts at `ano_GltfParser.c:23-34`, every `VkVertexInputAttributeDescription` and every shader) and only step 10 deletes the ModelAsset tree. The recon's risk register warns about this by name: 'Sequence skins/animations AFTER the renderer retains handles, or the two changes will fight in the same files.' Swap steps 9 and 10.

KILL `plan_hook`, OR MAKE IT COMPILER-ENFORCED. SOA-PLANES admits 'the five models are pure data' is ~90% true and that `plan_hook` is 'the 10% where a contestant could smuggle in an advantage,' mitigated by 'a POLICY... enforced by review, not by the compiler.' B.2 exists to forbid exactly that: 'A hidden allocator cannot absorb a model's predicted wound.' Either delete the hook (make the route/kind_route tables total, refusing at `RES_ARENA_COUNT`) or constrain it to a signature that CANNOT invent a root — e.g. `int (*narrow)(const res_model*, const res_place_plan*, size_t, res_arena_id in, res_arena_id *out)` where `out` must be an arena already present in the root the table selected. A review policy is not an invariant and the contest's integrity should not rest on one.
fatal_flaws: NONE OF THE THREE FAILS THE FIVE-MODEL GATE. All three can host complete Model A/B/C/D/E with registry shards, names, metadata, payloads, derived objects and teardown records owned by the model's own hierarchy; none secretly forces per-lifetime-domain or role-split; all three correctly turn `transfer_compatible` from a caller-computed threshold into an OUTPUT of the model's `plan()`; all three forbid the default-heap escape by routing EVERY registry byte through the seam and deleting `root_plan`/`root_block_alloc`. Stating this plainly because the brief invited me to disqualify, and the honest answer is that the field cleared the bar. Also stating plainly: the packet promised FOUR architectures and delivered THREE.

MINIMAL-CORE — TELEMETRY FAILS B.2's CORRECTNESS GATE, ARCHITECTURALLY. Its attribution is a dense (kind x lifetime x role) cube plus a SEPARATE flat per-operation vector, not a four-way cross. Its own wound list says it: 'A weakness that exists only in one (kind x operation) cell — say, promotion of audio PCM specifically — can still hide.' B.2's requirement is verbatim 'Every allocation and free is attributed to a resource kind, lifetime, role, and operation SO AGGREGATE WINS CANNOT HIDE ONE CATASTROPHICALLY WEAK CLASS.' Stage A's entire purpose is to make Stage B's contest judgeable; a design that ships a cube which cannot see the cell where a model's wound lives has failed the gate that Stage A exists to erect. This is not a graft — the other two solved it (SOA-PLANES: 19-bit packed key -> 1024-cell open-addressed table with the cell resolved ONCE at plan time and cached in `res_site.cell`; CAPABILITY: the interned `charge` cell carried in the token itself). MINIMAL-CORE must adopt one of them or it cannot be the substrate.

MINIMAL-CORE — REOPENS ITS OWN TYPE-CONFUSION HOLE. It bans '#' from `byte_ok` and invents `res_rid_derived(src_rid, kind_tag)` — the best derived-identity primitive in the packet — and then, in the same document, mints DUPLICATE identities as the string `"<logical>@<owner>"` with '@' left legal in the path grammar. A real file named `textures/x.png@3` is then rid-identical to the duplicate of `textures/x.png` in domain 3, and `ano_res_get` will happily bind it. This is the exact hole (`'#'` is a legal path byte, so `ano_res_get(lt, "models/viking_room.gltf#gfx")` serves the scene block as raw bytes) that the recon calls 'a live correctness hole, not just a tidiness issue' — closed on one door and left open on the next.

CAPABILITY — THE MINT-TIME ALIGNMENT REFUSAL IS UNSOUND. `res_cap_mint` is specified to refuse when 'NO arena the active placement offers for this tuple can deliver req->alignment.' `res_cap_req` contains `{lifetime, kind, role, operation, destination, disposition, alignment}` and NO SIZE. But the multipool's delivered alignment is a function of the size class — verified: `ano_mem_multipool_alloc(ano_mem_multipool *mp, size_t size)` (anoptic_memory_pools.h:134) takes no alignment at all and the header documents alignment as min(class stride, 4096). A 20-byte payload asking for ANO_CACHE_LINE lands in the 32-byte class at 32-byte alignment; a 4096-byte payload in the same arena gets 4096. So the mint-time check cannot be evaluated. Every model's `plan()` already ends `return o->alignment >= res_cap_alignment(c) ? 0 : -1;` — that one is correct. Delete the mint-time claim; the architecture's headline ('illegal placement is unrepresentable, not merely rejected late') is overstated by exactly this much.

CAPABILITY — `c_wink` DANGLES `model_state`. `c_open` allocates the model's own control struct INSIDE the heap it will later destroy: `c_dom *d = mi_heap_calloc(root, 1, sizeof *d); ... *b = (res_backing){ .root = root, ..., .model_state = d };` and `c_wink` then does `mi_heap_destroy(b->root); b->root = NULL;` and afterwards reads `b->transfers_outstanding` and may still touch `b->transfer_root`. `b->model_state` is never nulled and now points into a destroyed heap; any subsequent `arena_stats(b, arena)` — which the per-domain residual accessor B.5/B.7 are judged on — or any deferred-transfer path dereferences it. Model E has the identical shape.

CAPABILITY — SHIPS AN ALLOCATOR WITH NO CONSUMER, AND SAYS SO. Its own wound list: 'ano_mem_stripe ships in Stage A with a home bench and a wired RES_ARENA_STRIPE, but under the two TRUTHFUL scaffolds nothing routes to it... That is a roster item without a Stage-A production consumer, which is precisely what A.2 Allocator roster complains about for ano_mem_pool. I refuse to invent a fake consumer.' The refusal to fake one is admirable; the conclusion is wrong. A.2 does not say 'omit no promised tool UNLESS you cannot find a consumer' — and a consumer was there to be found. `g_readers[64]` is a static array of 16-byte `{_Atomic uint32_t cookie; _Atomic uint64_t epoch;}` lanes — FOUR PER CACHE LINE — raced by 1.2M reader observations in anotest_resownership. That is a live false-sharing bug and a genuine stripe consumer, and only SOA-PLANES found it.

ALL THREE — MODEL B's WHOLE-KIND TEARDOWN IS A USE-AFTER-FREE. `res_place_b_kind_wink(k)` / `res_placement_b_retire_kind(k)` / SOA-PLANES's 'whole-kind teardown IS a mi_heap_destroy' each destroy a kind's root heap without invalidating the publication directory and without crossing the epoch grace barrier that `queue_retire_locked`/`reader_safe`/`collect_locked` already implement. A reader inside an open `ano_res_read` scope holding a `res_pub` whose block lives in that heap reads freed memory. This is Model B's HOME-GROUND measurement — the subsystem-restart number it exists to win — and as written all three would measure it on a crash. It is not disqualifying because it is uniformly wrong and uniformly fixable, but the winner must run the identical retire sequence, and CAPABILITY's scoping ('the contest harness calls this, production never does') is the only honest framing of the three.

CAPABILITY + MINIMAL-CORE — MODEL B's KIND-MAJOR REGISTRY IS A FICTION AT CHUNK GRANULARITY. CAPABILITY: 'Each domain owns `res_shard_row **shard_chunks`.' MINIMAL-CORE: `res_binding *chunks[RES_SHARD_CHUNKS]` in 32-binding chunks with `res_owned_block chunk_storage[RES_SHARD_CHUNKS]`. A chunk is ONE allocation and therefore carries ONE capability and ONE kind — but it holds bindings of MANY kinds. So under Model B, the 'kind-major shard' is a block of mixed-kind rows sitting in one arbitrary kind's metadata multipool. B.4 forbids this by name ('never a tag in the Model A pool') and B.1 forbids it again ('changing only one payload pointer is not a contestant'). Neither squad noticed. Only SOA-PLANES's per-binding `res_bind * *plane_bind[C]` plane avoids it, and that fact is why it wins.

scores.architecture: SQUAD SOA-PLANES: placement is a plane destination, not an allocator object graph
scores.note: The only submission that reads like it was written by someone who intends to MEASURE something. Three findings no one else made, all verified: (1) g_readers is a 16-byte-lane static array, 4 lanes per cache line, raced 1.2M times — live coherence pathology on the exact read path Stage C is built on, and they route it to ano_mem_stripe. (2) RES_PLANE_GRAIN=64 is a compile-time constant deliberately NOT ANO_CACHE_LINE, because a baked block's interior offsets are an ABI and cannot track a 128-byte arm64 line — sharp, correct, and it buys 64-aligned plane bases inside every conditioned block where the other two leave them at 16. (3) ano_mem_stripe_planes(count, elem_size[], n_planes, out_planes) — 'N parallel arrays from ONE parent acquisition, every base on the grain' — is LITERALLY C.3's requirement ('control is SoA... compact ticket values occupy a separate plane'). It is the only design that pre-positions the SPMC instead of promising to invent it. Lowest hot-path tax: res_owned_alloc(plan, size, out) keeps its EXACT signature; the telemetry cell is resolved once in res_place_plan and cached in res_site.cell. Planar per-channel audio PCM is the SoA thesis paying rent inside a decoder. THE DEFECT, and it is ironic: the telemetry counters are SoA planes (cell_alloc_bytes[N], cell_serving[N], cell_allocs[N]...), so ONE allocation scatters ~3 bumps across 3 separate 8 KiB arrays = 3 cache lines, where a 64-byte AoS counter row touches 1. They applied their own thesis to a per-event scatter-update, where SoA is exactly wrong, and the cost lands on the alloc path B.8 microbenchmarks FIRST. One-file fix in resources_tel.c. Second defect: plan_hook is a declared model escape hatch policed by review — that is the private shortcut B.1 forbids.

scores.architecture: MINIMAL-CORE: a three-verb resource core with pluggable extensions over a placement vtable
scores.note: Loses by 0.1 and wins modularity by a mile. res_ext (fourcc kinds + classify/derive/validate/deps_of/share_policy) DELETES the res_kind enum and kind_from_path's switch — the guaranteed four-way merge collision the recon ledger names in every single area's integration_points. That is the best answer in any submission to the coordination problem, and the other two merely schedule it ('land it once, early'). res_shard_alloc() routing names/deps/records through the SAME plan() with role=REGISTRY is the cleanest statement of B.1's 'changing only one payload pointer is not a contestant'. ano_res_parcel {data,size,token,_site[4]} + ano_res_parcel_zero_copy() is exactly C.7's 'counted transfer token—not an already-retired ordinary handle', and it puts B.6's pointer-equality oracle IN THE PUBLIC API. It is the ONLY design that did the telemetry cache arithmetic honestly: 86 KiB / 2048 cells = 42 B/cell = ONE cache line per allocation. Also the only one to make the chunk pool CONTESTED (resources_stream.c asks the active placement for a chunk instead of owning an ano_mem_pool) — so B.6's 'bounded chunk reuse' home ground is a real contest. WHY IT LOSES ON MY LENS: it bought that one cache line by DROPPING THE OPERATION AXIS. The cube is (kind x lifetime x role); operation is demoted to a flat vector. B.2's sentence is 'attributed to a resource kind, lifetime, role, AND operation so aggregate wins cannot hide one catastrophically weak class.' They concede it in their own wounds: 'promotion of audio PCM specifically can still hide.' A contest whose product is the attribution table cannot ship a table that admits a wound can hide in it. Second hazard: res_kind_of(tag) dense ids depend on registration order, and Model B's ROOT AXIS IS THE DENSE KIND ID (b_kind g_b[RES_KIND_MAX], per-kind class-tuned multipools) — a registration-order change silently RETUNES Model B and makes every per-kind table incomparable across builds, against B.9's reproducibility bar. No stripe consumer in Stage A.

The thesis is refuted by its own code. `res_cap res_cap_mint(const res_cap_req *req)` with `res_cap_req { ...; size_t alignment; }` — THERE IS NO SIZE FIELD. A multipool's delivered alignment is min(class_size, 4096) (verified, anoptic_memory_pools.h:120), a function of SIZE. So mint CANNOT enforce its own stated rule ('Mint refuses when NO arena can deliver req->alignment. Illegal placement is therefore unrepresentable, not merely rejected late'), and sure enough every model they wrote ends its plan() with the LATE check `return o->alignment >= res_cap_alignment(c) ? 0 : -1;`. It IS rejected late. The 16-byte token is then dead weight threaded by value through every alloc, copy, move and free, with an authority-table lookup and a charge-intern hash bolted onto each — a fixed constant added to EVERY contestant's per-alloc cost, which COMPRESSES the relative spread between the five models on precisely the 'allocation microstructure' battlefield B.8 lists first. That is textbook bookkeeping distorting the contest. Worse: RES_CHARGE_MAX = 256 interned cells with overflow into cell 0. The realized tuple space (19 kinds x 7 lifetimes x 7 roles x 9 ops x 5 dests) plausibly lights 150-250 cells under a full level-cycle + pack + audio + script run, so the table's capacity is the SAME ORDER as its load — and on overflow the attribution for the overflowed tuples collapses into a lossy bucket, which is the exact 'one catastrophically weak class hides in the aggregate' failure B.2 exists to prevent. A decisive contest metric that can silently degrade is not a metric. They also concede the shared-immutable alias edge is 'a refcount wearing a nicer hat' — the weakest reading of A.4 bullet 7 of the three (SOA-PLANES' engine-lived SHARED_IMMUTABLE needs no count at all). And they ship ano_mem_stripe with ZERO Stage-A consumers and say so proudly — refusing to look is not the same as there being nothing there; SOA-PLANES found four. TWO REAL GIFTS: per-domain `backing.transfer_root` that outlives root (better than a process-wide transfer heap), and `res_free_mode {RETAIL, WINK}` + `backing.root may be NULL`, the tidiest way to host winkable and retail models behind one core retire order.

winner: SQUAD SOA-PLANES: placement is a plane destination, not an allocator object graph
rationale:
First, the record: FOUR squads were announced; THREE architectures arrived. I scored the three that exist.

None of the three fails the host-all-five gate. All three route registry shards, names, dep records, payloads, derived objects and teardown records through one seam keyed by the model's own hierarchy, so B.1's 'changing only one payload pointer is not a contestant' is satisfiable in each. All three delete serving_size(), make transfer_compatible an OUTPUT of plan() instead of a caller-computed 1 MiB threshold duplicated at three sites, split res_row into a permanent identity record plus a domain shard, add rid2 so collision refusal never memcmps a name in a retired domain, close the silent cross-lifetime alias at ano_res_get's hit path, and fix the anotest_resbench self-comparison. That convergence is the recon ledger talking, not three independent insights, and it means the decision turns entirely on what is NOT shared.

My lens is performance and measurability: which design lets Stage B SEE the truth, and which one's bookkeeping distorts the thing it measures. On that axis it is not close, even though the composite is 8.4 vs 8.3.

SOA-PLANES is the only submission that found a live cache pathology in the existing tree. I verified it: res_reader_lane is 16 bytes, g_readers is a plain static array of 64 of them — four lanes per cache line — and anotest_resownership hammers it with 1.2M reader observations. Two of three squads walked straight past a coherence bug sitting on the read path that C.4 and C.3 are built on. SOA-PLANES routes those lanes onto ano_mem_stripe and thereby gives the fourth allocator a genuine Stage-A production consumer, satisfying A.2's roster gate ('give every shipped allocator a real resource consumer and home benchmark') — where CAPABILITY and MINIMAL-CORE both ship stripe DEAD and CAPABILITY says so with a straight face. And it is the only design whose plane primitive, ano_mem_stripe_planes(count, elem_size[], n_planes, out_planes), is literally the thing C.3 demands: N parallel grain-aligned arrays from one parent acquisition, control plane separate from payload plane. The other two leave Stage C's cache-striped SPMC to be invented later; this one hands it an allocator.

Its one real performance defect is self-inflicted and ironic: the telemetry counters are SoA planes, so one allocation scatters three counter bumps across three separate 8 KiB arrays — three cache lines where a 64-byte AoS counter row touches one. SoA is the right layout for a bulk scan and the wrong layout for a per-event scatter-update, and the cost lands on the alloc path B.8 microbenchmarks first. MINIMAL-CORE got this right and did the arithmetic out loud (86 KiB / 2048 cells = one line per alloc). That is a one-file fix and it is graft #1.

MINIMAL-CORE lost the 0.1 on a self-declared measurability hole, not on engineering. Its core is the cleanest thing anyone submitted — res_ext kills the res_kind enum, which is the merge collision every area of the ledger flags; res_shard_alloc is the sharpest statement of the B.1 rule; ano_res_parcel is the best consume grammar in the pile and it puts B.6's zero-copy oracle in the public API. But it bought its one-cache-line cube by dropping the OPERATION axis, and B.2's sentence names four axes precisely so a wound cannot hide. Conceding 'promotion of audio PCM can still hide' in your own wounds list is conceding that the contest can be won dishonestly. Combined with dense kind ids that depend on registration order — while Model B's ROOT AXIS IS THE KIND ID — it has two independent ways for the comparison table to lie, and B.9 requires the decision be reproducible.

CAPABILITY is refuted by its own signatures. res_cap_mint takes an alignment and no size, while multipool alignment is min(class_size, 4096); mint therefore cannot enforce the refusal that is the architecture's entire reason to exist, and their own model code does the check late in plan() anyway. What remains is a 16-byte token, an authority lookup and an intern hash added to every allocation — a fixed tax that compresses the spread between the five models on the very microbenchmark the spec lists first — plus a 256-cell charge table whose capacity is the same order as its realized load and whose overflow bucket is exactly where a weak class would hide. It contributes two excellent mechanisms and one bad abstraction.

SOA-PLANES wins because its bookkeeping is the cheapest and its attribution is the most complete, because it is the only one that measured the machine it is standing on, and because it pre-positions Stage C instead of promising to. The margin is 0.1, which means the grafts below are not garnish — they are load-bearing, and the winner is incomplete without them.
grafts: TELEMETRY, from MINIMAL-CORE — THE HIGHEST-VALUE GRAFT. Keep SOA-PLANES' 19-bit five-axis packed key (kind|lifetime|role|op|dest) and the res_site.cell caching — that is complete B.2 attribution and it is strictly better than MINIMAL-CORE's three-axis cube. Replace ONLY the storage: swap the SoA counter planes (cell_alloc_bytes[N], cell_serving[N], cell_allocs[N], ...) for ONE 64-byte-aligned AoS counter row per interned cell, so a single allocation touches ONE cache line instead of three. This yields the combination none of the three achieved: full five-axis attribution AND one-line-per-alloc cost. Without it, the winner's own accounting perturbs the allocation microstructure benchmark B.8 lists first.

PER-DOMAIN mi_heap PAGE FOOTPRINT — THE GAP NOBODY CLOSED. Verified: ano_res_stats() (resources_registry.c:996-1004) sums only ano_mem_multipool_stats(d->pool).chunk_bytes. The domain mi_heap_t's retained pages are invisible, and today >1 MiB payloads are not even IN d->heap (owned_alloc_locked:163 sends transfer_compatible blocks to mi_malloc_aligned on the calling thread's DEFAULT heap). 'Residual footprint after every cycle' is B.5/B.7's decisive metric and B.2 demands it from EVERY model — and all three submissions ship a residual number that is short by every page mimalloc holds. Instrument the ano_mem_parent acquire/release seam ONCE (this also delivers B.2's 'parent calls' line item) and/or walk the heap, so every arena's parent traffic is charged to its domain root. NO residual figure may be quoted before this lands, or C-vs-E-vs-A is decided on a number that is structurally wrong.

res_ext, from MINIMAL-CORE, WHOLESALE. Fourcc kinds + classify/derive/validate/deps_of/share_policy + res_ext_register. It deletes the res_kind enum and kind_from_path's switch — the guaranteed four-way merge collision named in EVERY area's integration_points. SOA-PLANES keeps the enum and offers a coordination plan ('land it once, early'), which is a promise, not a fix. Adding .wav must touch src/resources/audio/res_audio.c and nothing else. GRAFT IT WITH ITS OWN HAZARD FIXED: a fixed res_ext_all[] registration order plus a static_assert freezing the dense-id-to-fourcc map, because the winner's Model B roots on the kind axis and a registration-order shift would silently retune Model B's per-kind multipool classes and make every cross-build per-kind table incomparable (B.9).

ano_res_parcel, from MINIMAL-CORE, replacing ano_res_transfer_free(void *data). {void *data; size_t size; uint64_t token; uint32_t _site[4];} + ano_res_take + ano_res_parcel_free + ano_res_parcel_zero_copy(). This IS C.7's 'counted transfer token—not an already-retired ordinary handle', and ano_res_parcel_zero_copy() puts B.6's pointer-equality ownership oracle in the public API where the bench can call it. SOA-PLANES' ano_res_transfer_free takes a BARE POINTER and has to look it up in a side table — it re-creates the raw-pointer hand-off the spec is trying to kill, and it cannot answer 'was that zero-copy' without a lookup.

backing.transfer_root, from CAPABILITY, replacing SOA-PLANES' single process-lived res_placement.transfer_heap. Per-domain, outlives root, destroyed at the next ano_res_collect after the last transfer returns. SOA-PLANES admits its process-wide transfer heap 'is the one thing a level wink-out does not reclaim' and downgrades the failure to 'a loud, accounted leak'. Per-domain transfer roots keep transfers ATTRIBUTABLE TO A DOMAIN — which the residual-footprint metric requires — and turn the leak into a bounded, retryable teardown. Combine with MINIMAL-CORE's outstanding_parcels barrier and retire returning -2, which is the same retryable shape ano_res_shutdown already uses for stalled readers.

res_free_mode {RES_FREE_RETAIL, RES_FREE_WINK} + 'backing.root may be NULL', from CAPABILITY, replacing SOA-PLANES' needs_teardown_records flag + branch. It is the tidiest mechanism for hosting winkable (C/E) and retail (A/B/D) models behind ONE invariant core retire order with zero `if (model == ...)` in resources_registry.c. Enforce it with MINIMAL-CORE's Debug post-wink assertion: a domain's live_bytes must return to zero after wink(), so a model that LIES about WINKS_WHOLE fails loudly instead of double-freeing or leaking quietly.

CONTESTED CHUNK POOL, from MINIMAL-CORE. resources_stream.c must NOT own an ano_mem_pool; it must ASK the active placement for a chunk (role=STAGING, dest=CHUNK), so D/E route it to a bounded ano_mem_pool and A routes it to its one multipool — and B.6's 'bounded chunk reuse' home ground becomes an actual contest rather than a shared primitive every model inherits. SOA-PLANES CONTRADICTS ITSELF here: its Model A arena roster has no CHUNK arena at all, but its streaming_pack section hardcodes RES_ARENA_CHUNK = ano_mem_pool(512 KiB, max_blocks 8) inside resources_stream.c. Resolve in favor of the routing table and delete the hardcoded pool.

DELETE plan_hook. SOA-PLANES ships a per-model C function pointer that can override the routing table and defends it with 'a policy is weaker than an invariant and we are not pretending otherwise.' In a contest that is the private shortcut B.1 forbids by name ('No favored model receives private shortcuts'). If a model genuinely cannot be phrased by (root_axis, arena roster, route plane, kind_route, small_max), that is evidence the TABLE is under-specified — extend the table for everyone, in the open, and re-run every model against it.

ano_mem_multipool_cfg NEEDS A CLASS LIST, or Model B is under-built and none of the three noticed. Verified: the config is {size_t min_block; size_t max_block;} with geometric power-of-two classes only (anoptic_memory_pools.h:123-126). B.4 requires that a kind's 'class-specific size histogram may tune multipool classes.' A min/max window is NOT a histogram-tuned class set, and all three squads propose tuning Model B via min_block/max_block alone. Add an explicit class-list (or class_stride/class_count) option before Stage B, or Model B fights the contest without the one capability its own model definition promises, and loses on ground it was never given.

res_cap_from_plan(), from CAPABILITY — the TECHNIQUE, not the token. Introduce the new placement vocabulary behind a temporary adapter so every existing call site keeps compiling unchanged, then retire the adapter in a separate commit. SOA-PLANES' step 2 changes res_place_plan's fields and breaks anotest_resownership.c:151 (which calls res_owned_alloc directly) and anotest_resbench.c in the SAME commit. The adapter makes that step green with zero test churn, which matters because the migration's whole discipline is 'the tree is GREEN at the end of every step.'
fatal_flaws: NONE OF THE THREE FAILS THE HOST-ALL-FIVE GATE, and I will not manufacture one. Each can express Model A (single root, one multipool, no wink), B (root per kind), C (root per lifetime, wink), D (root per role, retail), and E (lifetime root x role split). Each routes registry shards, names, dependency records, payloads, derived objects and teardown records through the same seam keyed by the model's own hierarchy. The items below are FATAL AS WRITTEN — they must be fixed before the contest can produce a valid result — but none of them disqualifies its architecture from the contest.

ALL THREE: no per-domain mi_heap page-footprint accounting. ano_res_stats() (resources_registry.c:996-1004) sums only ano_mem_multipool_stats(d->pool).chunk_bytes; the domain heap's retained pages are invisible, and today every >1 MiB payload is mi_malloc_aligned on the CALLING THREAD'S DEFAULT HEAP (owned_alloc_locked:163), not in d->heap at all. 'Residual footprint after every cycle' is B.5/B.7's home ground and B.2 demands it from every model. All three ship a residual number short by every page mimalloc holds. FATAL to the contest's decisive metric. Nothing may be quoted from it until fixed.

CAPABILITY: res_cap_mint(const res_cap_req *req) — res_cap_req carries `size_t alignment` and NO SIZE FIELD, while a multipool's delivered alignment is min(class_size, 4096) (anoptic_memory_pools.h:120), a function of SIZE. Mint therefore cannot enforce its own stated contract ('Mint refuses when NO arena the active placement offers for this tuple can deliver req->alignment. Illegal placement is therefore unrepresentable, not merely rejected late'). Their own five models all end plan() with the LATE check `return o->alignment >= res_cap_alignment(c) ? 0 : -1;`. The architecture's load-bearing claim is FALSE BY ITS OWN CODE, and what remains is a 16-byte token taxing every allocation for nothing.

CAPABILITY: RES_CHARGE_MAX = 256 interned ledger cells with overflow into cell 0. The realized tuple space (19 kinds x 7 lifetimes x 7 roles x 9 operations x 5 destinations) plausibly lights 150-250 cells across a full level-cycle + pack + audio + script run — the table's CAPACITY IS THE SAME ORDER AS ITS LOAD. On overflow, attribution for the overflowed tuples collapses into a lossy bucket, which is precisely the 'one catastrophically weak class hides in the aggregate' failure B.2 exists to prevent. A contest metric that can silently degrade under load is not a contest metric. FATAL to the validity of any Stage B table it produces.

MINIMAL-CORE: the attribution cube is (kind x lifetime x role); OPERATION is demoted to a separate flat vector. B.2 says 'Every allocation and free is attributed to a resource kind, lifetime, role, AND operation so aggregate wins cannot hide one catastrophically weak class.' They concede the miss in their own wounds: 'a weakness that exists only in one (kind x operation) cell — say, promotion of audio PCM specifically — can still hide.' The design cannot answer the single query B.5/B.7's hostile ground is built on. FATAL as written; cured by adopting SOA-PLANES' 19-bit five-axis key (which the AoS-counter-row fix makes free).

MINIMAL-CORE: res_kind_of(tag) dense ids depend on extension REGISTRATION ORDER, and Model B's ROOT AXIS IS THE DENSE KIND ID (b_kind g_b[RES_KIND_MAX], indexed by res_kind_of, with per-kind class-tuned multipools). A registration-order change therefore SILENTLY RETUNES MODEL B'S ARENAS — not merely its stats — and makes every per-kind bench table incomparable across builds. B.9 requires the decision be 'reproducible across the required platforms.' FATAL to reproducibility as written; cured by a fixed res_ext_all[] order plus a static_assert on the id-to-fourcc map.

SOA-PLANES: the telemetry counters are SoA planes (uint64_t cell_alloc_bytes[N], cell_serving[N], cell_allocs[N], cell_frees[N], ...), so ONE allocation scatters ~3 counter bumps across 3 separate 8 KiB arrays = 3 cache lines, where a 64-byte AoS counter row per cell touches 1. The squad applied its own SoA thesis to a per-event scatter-update, the one access pattern where SoA is wrong, and the cost lands on the allocation path that B.8 lists as its FIRST battlefield. This is bookkeeping distorting the contest it measures. FATAL as written to the 'allocation microstructure' series; a one-file fix in resources_tel.c.

SOA-PLANES: plan_hook is a per-model function pointer that can override the routing table, defended as a review policy rather than an invariant. B.1: 'No favored model receives private shortcuts.' A contestant-supplied C hook that can re-route any plan IS a private shortcut, and 'enforced by review' is not enforcement. FATAL to contest fairness as written; delete plan_hook and extend the routing table in the open instead.
